description: Constructs, parses, and serializes FIX protocol messages with header, body, and trailer sections; supports repeating field groups and multi-version validation

actors.name: Session Layer
actors.description: Sends serialized message strings over the wire and receives raw byte streams

actors.name: Application Layer
actors.description: Constructs outgoing messages and processes incoming parsed messages

actors.name: Data Dictionary
actors.description: Validates field presence, types, and allowed values per FIX version

message_structure.Every FIX message has three sections: Header (BeginString, BodyLength, MsgType), Body (message-specific fields), and Trailer (Checksum)

message_structure: Fields must appear in the correct section; body fields placed in header are rejected when ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder=Y
Groups (repeating sets of fields) are introduced by a count field and a delimiter field defined in the DataDictionary
framing: Fields are separated by the SOH character (0x01)
BeginString (tag 8) must be the first tag; BodyLength (tag 9) must be the second; CheckSum (tag 10) must be the last
BodyLength must exactly equal the byte count between tag 9 and tag 10
CheckSum must equal the modulo-256 sum of all bytes before it
serialization: Serialization automatically recalculates BodyLength and CheckSum; manual assignment is ignored
Fields within each section are written in the order defined by the DataDictionary (or insertion order if dictionary absent)
PreserveMessageFieldsOrder setting keeps original order for pass-through scenarios
groups: Repeating groups begin with a count field (e.g. NoAllocs tag 78)
The first field of each group instance is the delimiter; fields are assigned to the correct instance based on it
Groups may be nested; the outer group count field determines how many inner group iterations exist
message_identification: Admin messages (Logon, Logout, Heartbeat, etc.) are handled by the session layer before application delivery
Application messages (NewOrderSingle, ExecutionReport, etc.) are delivered to the application via fromApp()

given: BeginString, BodyLength, or CheckSum is missing or invalid
invalid_framing.result: Message discarded; session-level Reject may be sent

given: computed checksum does not match tag 10 value
checksum_mismatch.result: Message rejected; session Reject sent with reason

given: a body field appears in the header, or header field in body
actual number of group instances does not match count field value
application requests a field from a message
field tag is not present in the message
field_not_found.result: FieldNotFound exception thrown to the application

given: parsed message has an admin MsgType (0,1,2,3,4,5,A)
admin_message_received.result: Message routed to session layer for protocol processing via fromAdmin() callback

given: application creates a new message with a valid MsgType
app_message_built.result: Message is serialized, persisted, and sent over the session connection

given: raw FIX string received from wire
framing, checksum, and field structure are valid
app_message_parsed.result: Structured Message object delivered to application via fromApp() callback

errors.message: Tag specified out of required order

events.name: fix.message.sent
events.description: Application message successfully serialized and queued for transmission

events.name: fix.message.admin_received
events.description: Administrative message received and routed to session layer

events.name: fix.message.app_received
events.description: Application message parsed and delivered to application layer

events.name: fix.message.rejected
events.description: Message rejected due to structural or validation failure

events.name: fix.message.parse_error
events.description: Raw message could not be parsed at all

related.reason: Session layer controls when messages are sent/received and maintains sequence numbers
DataDictionary validates field types, required fields, and value ranges on parsed messages
Outgoing messages are persisted before transmission for recovery purposes

goals.description: Constructs, parses, and serializes FIX protocol messages with header, body, and trailer sections; supports repeating field groups and multi-version validation

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details
